findings.title: MilestoneTier enum lives in entity/ but is not a JPA entity — misleading package convention
findings.suggested_fix: Move MilestoneTier to a non-entity package — either domain/seasoninsight/model/, domain/seasoninsight/dto/common/, or merge it into the resolver. Existing convention in domain/order/entity/, domain/workspace/entity/ etc. reserves entity/ for @Entity-annotated JPA aggregates (with the lone exception of GhostType.kt, which is a similar smell). The seasoninsight package has no JPA entities, so keeping a single non-persistent enum in entity/ creates a phantom 'this is a persistence module' signal that will mislead the next reader.

findings.title: Inconsistent dependency naming inside SeasonInsightService — abbreviations mixed with full names
findings.suggested_fix: Pick one convention and apply it consistently. The constructor mixes `insightRepo`, `metricsCalc` (abbreviated) with `distributionService`, `strengthSelector`, `tierResolver` (full). SeasonDistributionService also has `repo` + `metricsCalc`. Recommend full names everywhere: `seasonInsightRepository`, `seasonMetricsCalculator`. Repo-wide convention (e.g., WorkspaceFacade, OrderFacade) already uses full names for collaborators. Same fix applies to SeasonDistributionService.kt:13-14.

findings.title: MilestoneStateDto is a single-field wrapper — wrapper does not earn its keep
findings.suggested_fix: Either inline `currentTier: String?` directly into SeasonInsightResponse (the only consumer) or, if the wrapper is intentionally future-proofing for additional milestone fields (next tier, progress %, threshold), document that intent in a kdoc and rename to `MilestoneState` (drop the Dto suffix — it conveys no information when the type is already in dto/common/). Today the indirection is one field deep with one caller.

findings.title: dto/common/ contains internal computation types (WorkspaceSeasonAggregate, SeasonMetrics, SeasonDistribution) alongside response DTOs
findings.suggested_fix: Split internal value objects from API DTOs. WorkspaceSeasonAggregate (repository projection), SeasonMetrics (intermediate calculator output), and SeasonDistribution (cache payload) are not API contracts — they should live under e.g. domain/seasoninsight/model/ (or service/internal/), while StrengthDto, SocialProofDto, MilestoneStateDto, SeasonInsightResponse stay in dto/. Conflating them invites accidental API changes when refactoring internals and confuses readers about the public surface. Compare with domain/order/dto/common/ which holds API-facing OrderDto/OrderProductDto only.

findings.title: Duplicated QueryDSL aggregation between aggregateForWorkspace and aggregateAll
findings.suggested_fix: Both methods build the same five expressions (notCancelled, totalSales, totalOrders, cancelledOrders, businessDayCount, lastOrderAt) and the same tuple→aggregate mapping. Extract the expression-building and tuple-mapping into private helpers, or have aggregateForWorkspace simply call aggregateAll-with-filter via a shared core. Otherwise any change to the aggregation rule (e.g., excluding GHOST orders, fixing timezone of `cast as date`) must be made twice and the two paths will silently drift.

findings.title: Six-service decomposition for a single experimental read endpoint risks over-engineering
findings.suggested_fix: Given the explicit short-lived experimental nature ('여름축제 후원 실험용', likely deletable within ~1 month), evaluate whether MilestoneTierResolver (8 lines of logic), StrengthSelector (one map+filter+take), PercentileCalculator (single rank-and-ceil), and SeasonMetricsCalculator (four ratios) need to be separately injected @Component beans. Each split adds a Spring bean, a test class, and a wiring concern. Consider collapsing PercentileCalculator + MilestoneTierResolver + StrengthSelector + SeasonMetricsCalculator into one `SeasonInsightCalculator` (or even private functions on SeasonInsightService) — keep the SeasonDistributionService split because it owns a distinct cache lifetime (1h vs 5m). When the experiment is removed, deletion is one package vs. seven files plus seven tests. If the team prefers fine-grained testability, document that decision in package-info or in the plan.

findings.title: ClockConfiguration is a one-bean configuration with no domain home
findings.suggested_fix: Acceptable as-is, but if no other consumers appear, fold the @Bean into an existing global configuration (e.g., a TimeConfiguration that also exposes ZoneId, or directly into the application's main @Configuration). The bean name `systemClock` also leaks implementation detail — `clock` is the conventional bean name and matches injection sites (`private val clock: Clock`). Only flagging because the file currently exists solely to back one consumer (SeasonDistributionService); if Clock injection is being introduced as a project-wide testability convention, leaving it standalone is fine — please document that intent.

findings.title: Magic-number TTL suffixes in cache names (`#5m`, `#1h`) lack a single source of truth
findings.suggested_fix: The `#5m` / `#1h` convention is parsed elsewhere by the cache config (also used by RealTimeStatisticsStrategy:19) so this is an existing pattern, not a new one. Still: define `CacheNames.SEASON_INSIGHT_5M = "seasonInsight#5m"` constants so the parsed token lives next to its declaration in CacheNames, and so a grep-by-cache-region works without string interpolation. Also include the `#5m` form in `CacheNames.ALL` if the cache manager iterates that list to register regions; current ALL contains the bare names only — verify the cache manager actually registers `seasonInsight#5m` at runtime.

findings.title: Strength metric keys are stringly-typed (`"AVG_ORDER_AMOUNT"`, `"TURNOVER_RATE"`, …)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the four string literals with an enum (e.g., `StrengthMetric { AVG_ORDER_AMOUNT, TURNOVER_RATE, TABLE_REVENUE, LOW_CANCELLATION_RATE }`) and serialize via name. Today a typo on the FE/BE contract is a silent bug; with an enum both ends share the closed set and the compiler enforces exhaustiveness when a metric is added. StrengthDto.metric: String can become StrengthDto.metric: StrengthMetric (Jackson serializes by name).

findings.title: PercentileCalculator.percentileLong forces Long→Double allocation per call by mapping the whole list
findings.suggested_fix: `percentileLong` calls `distribution.map { it.toDouble() }` allocating a fresh List<Double> per resolve call. Either inline a Long-typed copy of the rank logic, or accept that the call site is once-per-request and add a comment noting the allocation is intentional. Maintainability concern: the delegation reads as 'free' but isn't, and a future caller with a hot loop will be surprised. A 6-line specialized version would be clearer than the type-laundering wrapper.

residual_risks: Experimental feature lifecycle: branch name `feat/season-insight-donation` and Tag description ('여름축제 후원 실험용') signal a short-lived feature. The 7-file/7-test decomposition makes the eventual deletion non-trivial — confirm with the owner that the package boundary is intentional so removal is a single `rm -r domain/seasoninsight/` rather than a hunt-and-peck.

Cache invalidation: SeasonInsightService caches per-workspace for 5m and SeasonDistributionService caches globally for 1h. When an order is created/cancelled, neither cache is evicted via OrderCacheEvictListener (verified by grep). Stale cumulativeSales for up to 5m is acceptable for an admin dashboard, but worth an explicit comment so the next maintainer doesn't 'fix' it by hooking into OrderUpdateEvent without measuring the cost.

MilestoneTier enum names (`TOP_50`, `TOP_30`, `TOP_10`) leak into the API as raw strings (SeasonInsightService.kt:36 — `currentTier?.name`). Renaming the enum is now a breaking API change. If the FE contract is supposed to be stable, freeze the names with @JsonValue or a string mapping rather than relying on `.name`.
testing_gaps: No test asserts the duplication risk in SeasonInsightRepository — both aggregateForWorkspace and aggregateAll should produce equivalent aggregates for the same workspace, but no equivalence test exists. A single parameterized test would prevent silent drift between the two paths.

No integration test verifies the `seasonInsight#5m` and `seasonDistribution#1h` cache regions are actually registered by the cache manager. CacheNames.ALL lists the bare names only; if the cache config doesn't expand the `#TTL` suffix, @Cacheable will fall back to a no-op or a default region without warning.

No test asserts that MilestoneTier.fromPercentile boundary behavior matches the API contract for `percentile == 0` (current code returns TOP_10 — confirm intentional given PercentileCalculator coerces minimum to 1) and for `percentile > 50` (returns null, but no test exercises percentile = 51 to lock in the boundary).
